commit.message:
Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern (#3561)

* Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern

The tvOS native build was combining device and simulator binaries into a
single fat framework at output/native/tvos/, while the NuGet packaging
only included runtimes/tvos/native/ — missing runtimes/tvossimulator/.
This caused linker errors when building for tvOS simulator.

Changes:
- native/tvos/build.cake: Split output into tvos/ and tvossimulator/
  directories, add ios_use_simulator flag, pass SKIA_PLATFORM property
- SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile
- HarfBuzzSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile

This mirrors the proven iOS build pattern that already correctly splits
device (ios/) and simulator (iossimulator/) native assets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix tvOS GnNinja to use min_tvos_version instead of min_ios_version

The tvOS build was incorrectly passing min_ios_version to GnNinja, which set
the iOS deployment target but left the tvOS deployment target at Skia's default
of 9.0. This changes it to min_tvos_version so the -mtvos-version-min flag is
set correctly to 11.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SkiaSharp
repository.description: SkiaSharp is a cross-platform 2D graphics API for .NET platforms based on Google's Skia Graphics Library. It provides a comprehensive 2D API that can be used across mobile, server and desktop models to render images.

commit.message:
fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- NuxtLink for internal navigation with absolute paths
- Main content preserved exactly from source HTML

Bug fixes found while making tests pass:

  jsapi_ticket→js_api_ticket to match GORM snake_case column generation

* refactor: replace config files with env vars; remove Dockerfile.api-test and tests/api/config.yaml

  vars, override healthcheck to keep fast poll timing

  optional (skip ReadInConfig when configPath is empty)

Co-authored-by: luoxiaojun1992 <6905700+luoxiaojun1992@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add SetEnvKeyReplacer so nested APP_* env vars are read correctly

Viper's AutomaticEnv() upper-cases keys as-is, producing APP_DATABASE.HOST
(with a literal dot) instead of APP_DATABASE_HOST. Without a replacer, the
env var lookup always fails and the default value (localhost) is used.

Fix: v.SetEnvKeyReplacer(strings.NewReplacer(".", "_"))

Also adds config_test.go with tests for env-var-only and default-value loading.

Co-authored-by: luoxiaojun1992 <6905700+luoxiaojun1992@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

fix: scan report panel destroyed by updateLastScan textContent

Root cause: updateLastScan() used el.textContent which replaced ALL
child content including the scan-report-panel div. Now uses a text
node to update only the label text while preserving child elements.

Verified with Playwright: 14/14 tests pass — panel opens on click,
shows all 4 phases with status icons, task counts, retry info, error
details, duration, and warning indicator. Closes on second click.

commit.message:
fix: prevent grouping consecutive code blocks with the same language (#667)

* fix: prevent grouping consecutive code blocks with the same language

Two consecutive code blocks with the same language (e.g., both `mjs`)
were incorrectly grouped into a CJS/MJS switchable tab. Add a check
to ensure the two code blocks have different languages before creating
a switchable code tab.

Refs: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/62181

commit.message:
Fix falsy enum values returning None instead of name

AlarmReason.NO_ALARM has value 0, making it falsy in Python. The
_enum_to_lowercase function used 'if enum_value' which incorrectly
returned None for NO_ALARM. Changed to 'if enum_value is not None'
to properly handle zero-valued Flag enums.

This caused battery_monitor alarm and dc_energy_meter alarm to show
as 'unknown' instead of 'no_alarm' in Home Assistant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: scan report panel clickability + scan retry on timeout

Two fixes:

1. Scan Report Panel was not opening on click:
   - Moved panel div inside the last-scan span (correct DOM nesting)
   - Added event.stopPropagation() to prevent parent click interference
   - Fixed close-on-outside-click to check parent container

2. Scan Phase 1 timeout now retries with reduced context:
   - On first timeout (180s), creates fresh SDK session
   - Reduces task context from 80 to 20 tasks
   - Reduces scan range from N to min(N, 2) days
   - Logs both attempts with timing for diagnostics
   - If both fail, returns 502 with descriptive error

commit.message:
feat: expandable scan report panel with per-phase metrics

Clicking 'Last scan' now opens a detailed report panel showing:
- Phase 1 (Discovery): success/failure, items found/updated/skipped
- Phase 2 (Enrichment): tasks processed, success/failed/retried counts
- Phase 3 (Update Check): tasks checked, success/failed/retried counts
- Phase 4 (Consolidation): merge suggestions found
- Per-phase timing (duration in seconds)
- Error details for failed tasks (up to 5 shown)
- Warning indicator on 'Last scan' label if any phase had issues

Data collected in real-time during scan execution and persisted in
localStorage. Report survives page refreshes. Closes on click outside.

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: switch from Claude Opus 4.6 to default model for faster responses

Removed explicit 'claude-opus-4-6' model override from:
- Intent classification (analyzePrompt session)
- Correction verification session

This significantly reduces response time:
- Before (Opus): 26-39s per intent classification
- After (default): 12-19s per intent classification

Process isolation verified: 3 concurrent requests complete
independently with no data loss or interference.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add SystemVerilog testbenches for RTL primitives library

Create 16 standalone testbench files in testbenches/ directory covering:
- CDC: sync_2ff, async_fifo
- Arbiters: fixed_priority_arbiter, round_robin_arbiter
- Bitops: priority_encoder, popcount, barrel_shifter
- Counters: up_counter, gray_counter
- Memory: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo
- Handshake: skid_buffer
- Pipeline: pipeline_register, delay_line
- Encoding: gray_encoder
- Clock: clock_divider

Each testbench uses `timescale 1ns/1ps, waveform dumps, $error for
failures, PASS/FAIL summary, and tests basic functionality plus corner
cases such as reset, overflow, backpressure, and one-bit Gray transitions.
Testbenches assume DUT modules are compiled separately in the same
compilation unit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
lis2mdl: Potential fix for pull request finding.

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add pipeline primitives directory with 6 RTL modules

- pipeline_register: single stage with enable/flush
- delay_line: parameterized shift register with per-tap outputs
- latency_balancer: aligns two parallel paths via delay compensation
- pipeline_fork: 1-to-N valid/ready fork with completion mask
- pipeline_join: N-to-1 all-valid gate join
- pipeline_bubble_injector: NOP insertion / bubble injection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rtl-primitives
repository.description: Open Source RTL primitives library for FPGA and ASIC designers in SystemVerilog

commit.message:
Add memory RTL primitives: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo, register_file, cam, line_buffer, circular_buffer

- dual_port_ram: true dual-port RAM with independent clocks, registered-read for block RAM inference
- simple_fifo: synchronous FIFO with explicit count register for full/empty detection
- register_file: multi-read-port file with combinational reads and write-to-read forwarding
- content_addressable_memory: fully-associative CAM with priority-encoded hit address
- line_buffer: streaming image line buffer outputting a vertical column of NUM_LINES pixels
- circular_buffer: ring buffer with head/tail pointers, full/empty, and count output

All modules: snake_case signals, UPPER_CASE parameters, always_ff/always_comb,
no initial blocks, no vendor primitives, fully synthesizable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rtl-primitives
repository.description: Open Source RTL primitives library for FPGA and ASIC designers in SystemVerilog

commit.message:
Add counters/ directory with 8 synthesizable SystemVerilog primitives

- up_counter: synchronous up counter with enable, load, registered overflow
- down_counter: synchronous down counter with enable, load, registered underflow
- up_down_counter: bidirectional counter with overflow/underflow detection
- saturating_counter: clamps at MAX/MIN with combinational at_max/at_min flags
- gray_counter: binary counter with XOR Gray-code output for CDC use
- event_counter: event accumulator with threshold comparison and sync clear
- watchdog_timer: programmable down-counter watchdog with one-cycle timeout pulse
- programmable_interval_timer: configurable periodic/one-shot tick generator

All modules follow project conventions: snake_case signals, UPPER_CASE
parameters, always_ff/always_comb, active-low synchronous reset (rst_n),
no initial blocks, no vendor-specific primitives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add bitops/ RTL primitive modules

Add 9 fully-synthesizable combinational SystemVerilog modules:
- priority_encoder: lowest-index set bit to binary with valid flag
- leading_zero_counter: MSB leading zero count, width clog2(WIDTH+1)
- trailing_zero_counter: LSB trailing zero count, width clog2(WIDTH+1)
- popcount: adder-tree population count, O(log2 WIDTH) depth
- onehot_encoder: one-hot to binary via OR reduction
- onehot_decoder: binary to one-hot with bounds check
- thermometer_encoder: binary N to N-ones thermometer code
- barrel_shifter: logical/arithmetic left+right, cascaded-mux
- rotate_unit: left/right rotation, cascaded-mux with right->left conversion

All modules verified with pyslang 10.0.0 elaboration: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
arbiters: fix weighted_round_robin ptr rotation and remove redundant self-assign

- Rotate ptr past gnt (departing winner) rather than gnt_next; semantically
  clearer and correct since they are equal in that branch
- Remove redundant 'ptr <= ptr' self-assignment in the new-winner branch

repository.description: Open Source RTL primitives library for FPGA and ASIC designers in SystemVerilog

commit.message:
arbiters: add 7 synthesizable SystemVerilog arbiter primitives

- fixed_priority_arbiter: combinational, isolate-lowest-bit trick
- round_robin_arbiter: masked-priority rotating, registered output
- weighted_round_robin_arbiter: per-port credit counter, programmable weight
- masked_priority_arbiter: combinational with external disable mask + fallback
- tree_arbiter: binary tree O(log N) depth, generate/assign for clean linting
- fair_rotating_arbiter: one-hot ptr advances every grant cycle
- parking_arbiter: sticky grant held while owner's request is asserted

All modules are fully synthesizable, vendor-primitive-free, and pass
Verilator --Wall lint with no warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rtl-primitives

commit.message:
Add handshake RTL primitives (7 SystemVerilog modules)

Introduces a new handshake/ directory with 7 fully synthesizable
SystemVerilog modules implementing common ready/valid handshake patterns:

- ready_valid_stage: single registered pipeline stage with back-pressure
- skid_buffer: 2-entry elastic buffer with registered in_ready
- elastic_buffer: circular FIFO (power-of-2 depth) with combinational head output
- mailbox_fifo: single-entry mailbox supporting simultaneous wr+rd
- credit_flow_controller: sender-side credit counter with registered output
- pipeline_register_slice: fully registered slice (EMPTY/FULL/BOTH FSM)
- backpressure_adapter: absorbs always-valid source into FIFO with overflow flag

All modules follow project conventions: snake_case ports/signals,
UPPER_CASE parameters, always_ff for flip-flops, active-low rst_n,
no initial blocks, no vendor primitives, no combinational ready/valid loops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
